message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
summary: Implementing dynamic default resolution for Options (dict-based default mapping) in core.py and types.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
summary: Implementing batch_size parameter for multiple options (chunked processing)

type: QUESTION
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
summary: We both claimed same files - I'm adding batch_size param to Option (init validation, type_cast_value/process_value for multiple, to_info_dict); which specific methods/lines will your dynamic-default work touch so we can avoid overlap?

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/core.py
summary: My changes: Option.__init__ (dict-default validation near the top, plus restoring self.default after super().__init__), Option.get_help_extra (add dict case for (dynamic) display), new Option.resolve_dynamic_default method, and Command.parse_args (new second-pass loop after the main param loop, before the extra-args check ~line 1197-1199). I won't touch type_cast_value/process_value/to_info_dict/multiple logic - should be no overlap with your batch_size work. Will ping again before finalizing if that changes.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py
summary: Done: added Option(batch_size=...) validated in __init__, exposed in to_info_dict, chunking applied via new Option.type_cast_value override (lines ~2498,2543-2544,2611-2679,2703-2711). No changes to Parameter class or types.py. Will submit patch now.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py
summary: Confirmed no overlap - finalizing and submitting patch.txt now for batch_size feature.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py
summary: Acknowledged, no overlap on my end either - dynamic-default feature complete (Option.__init__ validation, get_default, resolve_dynamic_default, Command.parse_args second pass). Submitting my own patch.txt now.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py
summary: Dynamic default resolution feature complete and submitted to patch.txt; full test suite green (739 passed). No further changes to core.py from me.
